title: The ballpoint pen — How this popular item evolved
content_html:
The ballpoint pen — How this popular item evolved

Most of us have at least one, but how did this popular item evolve?

One morning in 1945, a crowd of 5,000 people jammed the entrance of Gimbels Department Store in New York. The day before, Gimbels had placed a full-page advertisement in the New York Times for a wonderful new invention, the ballpoint pen. The advertisement described the pen as “fantastic” and “miraculous.” Although they were expensive, $12.50 each, all 10,000 pens in stock were sold on the first day.

In fact, this “new” pen was not new at all. In 1888, John Loud, a leather manufacturer, had invented a pen with a reservoir of ink and a rolling ball. However, his pen was never produced, and efforts by other people to produce a commercially successful one failed too. The main problem was with the ink. If it was too thin, the ink leaked out of the pen. If it was too thick, it didn’t come out of the pen at all.

Almost fifty years later, in 1935, a newspaper editor in Hungary thought he spent too much time filling his pens with ink. He decided to invent a better kind of pen. With the help of his brother, who was a chemist, he produced a ballpoint pen that didn’t leak when the pen wasn’t being used. The editor was called Ladislas Biro, and it was his name that people would associate more than any other with the ballpoint pen.

By chance, Biro met Augustine Justo, the Argentinian president. Justo was so impressed with Biro’s invention that he invited him to set up a factory in Argentina. In 1943, the first Biros were produced.

Unfortunately, they were not popular, since the pen needed to be held in a vertical position for the ink to come out. Biro redesigned the pen with a better ball, and in 1944 the new product was on sale throughout Argentina.

It was a North American, Milton Reynolds, who introduced the ballpoint pen to the USA. Copying Biro’s design, he produced the version that sold so well at Gimbels. Another American, Patrick Frawley, improved the design and in 1950 began producing a pen he called the Papermate. It was an immediate success, and within a few years, Papermates were selling in their millions around the world.

questions.question: John Loud
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–5

Match each statement with the correct option, A–E.

Write the correct letter in boxes 1–5 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation: EN: Loud 'invented a pen with a reservoir of ink and a rolling ball' in 1888. VI: Loud 'đã phát minh ra một cây bút có ngăn chứa mực và một viên bi xoay' vào năm 1888.

featureStatements:
sold the first ballpoint pens in North America
wanted to make his job easier.
achieved international success with his pen.
 invented the first pen with a rolling ball.
asked someone to start a business in his country.

questions.questionHtml:
List of features

A  John Loud

B  Ladislas Biro

C  Augustine Justo

D  Milton Reynolds

E  Patrick Frawley

1_______ sold the first ballpoint pens in North America

2_______ wanted to make his job easier.

3_______ achieved international success with his pen.

4_______ invented the first pen with a rolling ball.

5_______ asked someone to start a business in his country.

questions.question: Ladislas Biro
questions.explanation: EN: Biro was a newspaper editor who 'thought he spent too much time filling his pens with ink' and wanted to make his job easier. VI: Biro là một biên tập viên báo chí, người 'nghĩ rằng mình đã mất quá nhiều thời gian để bơm mực cho bút' và muốn công việc của mình dễ dàng hơn.

questions.question: Augustine Justo
questions.explanation: EN: Justo, the Argentinian president, 'invited him to set up a factory in Argentina'. VI: Justo, tổng thống Argentina, 'đã mời ông ấy thành lập một nhà máy ở Argentina'.

questions.question: Milton Reynolds
questions.explanation: EN: Reynolds was a North American who 'introduced the ballpoint pen to the USA, copying Biro's design'. VI: Reynolds là một người Bắc Mỹ 'đã đưa bút bi vào Mỹ, sao chép thiết kế của Biro'.

questions.question: Patrick Frawley
questions.explanation: EN: Frawley produced the Papermate which 'was an immediate success, and within a few years... selling in their millions around the world'. VI: Frawley sản xuất dòng bút Papermate và nó 'đã thành công ngay lập tức, và trong vòng vài năm... bán được hàng triệu chiếc trên khắp thế giới'.

options: A. they couldn't get them anywhere else
B. they had been told how good the pens were
C. they had never seen a ballpoint pen before
D. they thought the price was good
questions.question: People went to Gimbels to buy a ballpoint pen because:
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
advertisement
fantastic
miraculous
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

The advertisement described the pen as "fantastic" and "miraculous".

Bước 3: Phân tích

EN: The advertisement told people the pen was wonderful.

VI: Quảng cáo nói cây bút rất tuyệt vời nên mọi người đổ xô đi mua.

=> B. they had been told how good the pens were.

- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**

options: A. Nobody wanted to buy one
B. It cost too much to produce them
C. They used too much ink
D. They didn't work properly
questions.question: Why were early ballpoint pens not produced commercially?
questions.explanation: EN: The main problem was the ink leaking or not coming out (they didn't work properly). VI: Vấn đề chính là mực bị rò rỉ hoặc không chảy ra (chúng hoạt động không đúng cách).

options: A. It didn't need to be filled with ink as often
B. It was designed by a chemist
C. The ink stayed in the pen until it was needed
D. It was easier to use
questions.question: Why was Ladislas Biro's pen better than earlier models?
questions.explanation: EN: Biro produced a pen 'that didn't leak when the pen wasn't being used' (ink stayed in until needed). VI: Biro đã tạo ra một cây bút 'không bị rò rỉ mực khi không sử dụng' (mực được giữ lại cho đến khi cần).

options: A. was produced in a factory owned by the Argentinian president
B. only worked if used in a certain way
C. was a major success
D. went on sale in 1944
questions.question: Biro's first commercially-produced pen:
questions.explanation: EN: They were not popular because 'the pen needed to be held in a vertical position for the ink to come out' (needed to be used in a certain way). VI: Chúng không phổ biến vì 'cây bút cần phải được giữ ở vị trí thẳng đứng thì mực mới chảy ra được' (chỉ hoạt động nếu dùng theo cách nhất định).

options: A. was a better version of an earlier model
B. took time to become successful
C. was the USA's first commercially successful ballpoint pen
D. was only successful in the USA
questions.question: Patrick Frawley's pen:
questions.explanation: EN: Frawley 'improved the design' of the pen (a better version of an earlier model). VI: Frawley 'đã cải tiến thiết kế' của cây bút (một phiên bản tốt hơn của mẫu trước đó).
